Assembly: The assembly drUleMino1.hap1.1 is based on 111x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This assembly records a heterozygous inversion on Chromosome 3 from ~8.6-11.5 Mbp.

Assembly: The assembly daCymMura1.hap1.1 is based on 49x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This genome has been assembled using PacBio and HiC data and phased. The result is two curated haplotypes. A large number of centromeric repeats is in the unlocalised sequences. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 3 (35.4 - 36.6) are unknown.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Assembly: The assembly dhCarBetu1.hap6.1 is based on 92x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This sample is octaploid with basal chromosome number of 8. We have generated 8 curated haplotypes. Note that due to software limitations unplaced content is split between haplotypes 1 and 2 and haplotypes 6 to 8 only contain assembled chromosomes.

Assembly: The assembly rMedKot1.hap1.1 is based on 42x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas (ERGA, https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/) via the Biodiversity Genomics Europe project (BGE, https://biodiversitygenomics.eu/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This is an assembly from homogametic specimen. No close relative could be identified to reliably assign the Z chromosome by synteny.

Assembly: The assembly daCusEpit1.1 is based on 83x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Some inversions between the haplotypes were detected: 32.4 to 43 Mbp on Chromosome 2 and 36.6 to 54.56 Mbp on Chromosome 7. The region from the start to 6.2 Mbp on Chromosome 1 is highly repetitive and the order and orientation is uncertain.

Assembly: This project has been funded by the Federation Nationale des Chasseurs (FR). The assembly of Turdus iliacus (bTurIli2.1) is based on 51X ONT data and 35X Dovetail Hi-C data. The assembly process included the following steps: initial nanopore assembly generation with Nextdenovo, polishing of the consensus using Medaka (with long-reads) and Hapo-G (with short-reads), removal of contaminant sequences using Context, removal of haplotypic duplications using purge_dups, and Hi-C-based scaffolding with YaHS. No contigs were found to be contaminants (bacterial, archaeal, or viral). Additionally, 1,524 regions totaling 492 Mb (with the largest being 3.8 Mb) were identified as haplotypic duplications and removed. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using ptGAUL. Finally, the primary assembly was analyzed and manually improved using Pretext. During manual curation, 14 haplotypic regions were removed, totaling 4.20 Mb (with the largest being 898 Kb). Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by Hi-C data were named in order of size. Sexual chromosomes Z and W were identified using comparative genomics and organization of microchromosomes was curated using MicroFinder.

The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. the Z chromosome was identified by read coverage, no W chromosome was found. Closely related species are known to have Z0.

Assembly: The assembly ddPriVulg1.1 is based on 44x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly icGasPoly1.1 is based on 79x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. Assembly: The assembly rVipUrs1.1 is based on 28x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/) as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas pilot project~(https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/pilot-project). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using rapid curation. This alternate haplotype assembly combines the haplotigs separated by purge_dups with haplotigs separated from the primary assembly during curation. The order and orientation of chromosome W is not determined with full certainty.

Assembly: The assembly ilLycPhla1.2 is based on 55x PacBio data, 10X Genomics Chromium data, and Arima Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project (https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, short-read polishing using FreeBayes-called variants from 10X Genomics Chromium reads aligned with LongRanger, and Hi-C based scaffolding with SALSA. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHifi. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.
